textByHash.06551bc70a82678753f0b878d195f00219e8ceff: Hello again! Lovely day for it, isn't it?
textByHash.17d5235323d75a35752ac383ea93799ac23c6230: Nothing like a good day in Lovakengj, that's what I always say!
textByHash.2f05b579219a052adc6f0287d0ee7b93c2b80a27: Thankfully, with guidance from their brave leader, the settlers were able to overcome this evil! The Burning Man is a reminder of that.
textByHash.2f54a674298bbbe6abe45a57fc21e85afd35868d: You know, I heard some chap from Hosidius went missing not too long ago. They reckon he went exploring the Stranglewood. Poor guy.
textByHash.3da732c74b516aa01a180cf1ee6558d6d87ea01d: Ah, now that bit is less clear. Have you heard of the Stranglewood?
textByHash.99162ed7b8ee074d081f0d0ef7e61be2d6155593: Well you picked a great day for it! The name's Barus by the way. I work as a historian here. Those Arceuus lot may think they know it all, but I reckon I could tell you a thing or two that even they haven't realised!
textByHash.b04f107850b100e0bca1e16a2acfe6f0b93c35a2: Not too surprising. It's not a place you'd want to visit. Anyway, that's supposedly where this evil emerged from.
textByHash.b372e7ca38b5d1e91c72050a9286aab88090be40: The Burning Man is a monument to a great triumph in Lovakengj history. We're talking thousands of years ago, before Kourend was even a kingdom! Sadly, that does mean some details have been lost to time.
textByHash.b897d059c0c3740dd67206413a8ca714e4b80470: Some botanist I think. His name escapes me, but I think he lived just south of the Hosidius Estate Agent.
textByHash.bec38c68a38e04df54b4d8476ed0f011b7e7c3b3: If I remember right, he lived just south of the Hosidius Estate Agent.
textByHash.cdbe2fa9843965c9ea8a366118123c1117b730ed: You'll find it on the border between the Kebos Lowlands and the Kingdom of Varlamore. It's a cursed place. Few have ever entered, and of those that have, none have returned!
textByHash.e42a9dd4582fdc328c75693382774c449a7e7b5b: Hello! Here to see the Burning Man?
textByHash.fce61aaea8860a357ba6ad0d7e248e63f5a46310: What we do know is that a great evil threatened the earliest dwarven settlers here. Had that evil prevailed, Lovakengj might never have existed!
